title: Rethinking political thinkers [Book Review]
author: Sakurai

opening_move.description: The review opens by mapping the disciplinary landscape of global/comparative political theory, then locates the reviewed volume's place (and limits) within it.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Whether PT can genuinely broaden beyond its Western, Anglophone, male-dominated canon

theoretical_positioning.description: The review performs an immanent critique: accepting the editors' decolonisation premise, but showing that language parochialism undercuts it, and arguing for genuine CPT as a more consistent alternative.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: close reading of the volume's introduction and table of contents
epistemic_mode.notes: Argument proceeds entirely through normative and methodological critique; no empirical data deployed.

case_type.case_name: Ramgotra and Choat (eds.), Rethinking Political Thinkers (2024)

normative_stake.domain: methodology / disciplinary politics of political theory

confidence_notes: Book review: reviewer's own argument is a methodological critique. The 'fully_normative' classification reflects the reviewer's own methodological stance, not empirical engagement. The move sequence captures the review's argumentative logic.
